title: CDASH-aligned field names by default at the CDASH-fallback layer
description: Use CDASH-aligned field names by default for fields generated at the CDASH-default layer (level 3 of the §0 lookup hierarchy). Reference references/cdash-domain-library.md for the standard name per domain. Common examples: RF/RFICDAT (consent date); DM/BRTHDAT, AGE, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC (demographics); MH/MHTERM, MHSTDAT (medical history); AE/AETERM, AESTDAT, AESEV, AEREL, AEOUT (adverse events); VS/VSORRES, VSORRESU, VSDAT (vital signs); LB/LBORRES, LBORRESU, LBDAT, LBNAM (labs); DS/DSDECOD, DSSTDAT (disposition); IE/IETEST, IEORRES (eligibility criteria). When the customer library provides a descriptive English name like WRITTEN_IC_PRIOR, use it and tag the row with cdash_name_deviation=true plus cdash_standard_name=<CDASH equivalent>. Forms sourced from customer_oc4_standard or customer_crf_library are used as-authored — this convention does NOT override their content.
rationale: CDASH naming is the de facto standard across the clinical-trial industry. Field names that match CDASH translate cleanly into SDTM downstream, integrate with standard mapping tools, and produce data that's instantly recognizable to monitors and statisticians. Customer-preferred descriptive English names are honored when explicitly provided, but the default falls back to CDASH for any field the skill generates itself. The cdash_standard_name annotation on deviations keeps the SDTM mapping traceable.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} on form ${form.form_id} (definition_source=cdash_default) — verify name aligns with CDASH per references/cdash-domain-library.md for the form's cdash_domain. Common: RFICDAT, BRTHDAT, AGE, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC, MHTERM, MHSTDAT, AETERM, AESTDAT, AESEV, VSORRES, VSDAT, LBORRES, LBNAM, DSDECOD, IETEST, IEORRES. Customer-source overrides are exempt; tag those with cdash_name_deviation=true and cdash_standard_name=<equivalent>.

effect.soft: Look up each generated field's CDASH standard name in references/cdash-domain-library.md based on the form's cdash_domain. Apply the standard name unless the customer library has specified otherwise. The CDASH library is too large to enumerate exhaustively in the convention; the soft directive delegates the lookup to Claude, who has the library file accessible at build time.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §4 during Phase B.1d. Hybrid because the CDASH name lookup requires consulting references/cdash-domain-library.md per field — a per-domain lookup table large enough that inlining it as a match cases dict would be unmaintainable. Future work could promote this by either (a) extracting the cdash-domain-library into structured conventions per domain, or (b) extending the DSL with a 'lookup_from_external' operator that resolves against a named markdown table.
